title: Cold-Case Review: Diagnose Three Similar Failures
summary: Retrieve the foundation models without notes and separate path, response, parsing, tooling, and evidence-quality failures that share a visible symptom.
objectives: Select the responsible model from sparse mixed evidence.
Reject plausible alternatives and name the next discriminating observation.

steps.title: Distinguish internet and web · failure archive
steps.instruction: In the three-lookalike failure archive, which relationship is accurate?
steps.why: Local-file access and web requests can display similar pages but fail at different boundaries. Working through distinguish internet and web narrows the three-lookalike failure archive while you inspect the artifact.

content.title: Distinguish internet and web — three-lookalike failure archive
content.text: Commit to the claim that explains the three-lookalike failure archive evidence without assigning work to the wrong layer.

hints: During three-lookalike failure archive inspect, identify which load journey boundary “Distinguish internet and web” asks about.
Compare every three-lookalike failure archive option with the named “Distinguish internet and web” evidence during inspect.
For three-lookalike failure archive inspect, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Distinguish internet and web.”

options.text: The web is the physical cables, while the internet is only the pages displayed in browsers.
The internet supplies network infrastructure; the web uses it to exchange linked resources through web protocols.
Internet and web are exact synonyms with no useful distinction.

stimulus.title: Two notice loads · three-lookalike failure archive
stimulus.caption: Evidence from opening a saved file and visiting the published notice. Case: three-lookalike failure archive; focus: distinguish internet and web.

steps.title: Read the real filename · failure archive
steps.instruction: In the three-lookalike failure archive, the file manager shows schedule.html, but its type is Plain Text and hidden extensions are enabled. What should be checked?
steps.why: Portable paths preserve the relationship between project resources when the containing folder moves. Working through read the real filename narrows the three-lookalike failure archive as you connect the explanation to evidence.

content.text: A project is a directory tree. A filename includes its real extension, and a relative path describes one resource from the current document location. Keeping related resources inside the project and testing after moving the whole folder exposes machine-specific absolute paths and case mismatches. Apply this distinction during the three-lookalike failure archive read task.

hints: During three-lookalike failure archive read, identify which project paths boundary “Read the real filename” asks about.
Compare every three-lookalike failure archive option with the named “Read the real filename” evidence during read.
For three-lookalike failure archive read, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Read the real filename.”

options.text: Rename the containing folder to HTML so the operating system changes the file type.
Reveal full extensions because the file may actually be schedule.html.txt.
Assume the browser ignores extensions and opens every file as HTML.

steps.title: Choose structural evidence · failure archive
steps.instruction: In the three-lookalike failure archive, which artifact best answers what nodes currently exist?
steps.why: Source, the browser-built DOM, layout, and pixels are related stages rather than interchangeable evidence. Working through choose structural evidence narrows the three-lookalike failure archive before the result is shown.

options.text: A screenshot of the text color.
The inspected DOM tree.
The saved filename without its source contents.

steps.title: Rebuild system chain · failure archive
steps.instruction: Within the three-lookalike failure archive, reconstruct the system chain in causal order.
steps.why: Separating system responsibilities makes a visible symptom traceable to the layer that can cause it. Ordering the three-lookalike failure archive exposes its first unsupported causal handoff.

content.text: Do not order by vocabulary or visual position. Test whether each event creates the conditions required by the next event in the three-lookalike failure archive.

hints: In the three-lookalike failure archive sequence, locate the first event whose required input is not ready.
Separate three-lookalike failure archive system chain events from later visible outcomes.
Rebuild the three-lookalike failure archive sequence one causal handoff at a time.

options.text: Input hardware reports the open-file action
The operating system reads document bytes from storage
The browser parses the received bytes and prepares output
Graphics hardware and the display produce visible pixels

steps.title: Record uncertainty · failure archive
steps.instruction: In the three-lookalike failure archive, two current sources disagree and the test is inconclusive. What is the professional response?
steps.why: Technical guidance is safer when authority, currency, scope, and browser evidence agree. Working through record uncertainty narrows the three-lookalike failure archive while you defend the selected claim.

options.text: Choose the shortest source and delete evidence of the disagreement.
Document the conflict, environment, and uncertainty; avoid presenting the decision as settled fact.
Present the preferred answer as universal because uncertainty reduces confidence.

options.text: Elements, because DOM nodes generate every HTTP status.
Network, filtered to the stylesheet request and response status.
The color picker, because missing resources are color defects.

steps.title: Write the evidence note · failure archive
steps.instruction: Write a concise case note for the three-lookalike failure archive. Explain the mechanism, cite observed evidence, and reject one plausible alternative.
steps.why: Explaining the three-lookalike failure archive from memory exposes gaps that selected answers can hide. Cite the mechanism and its evidence.

content.title: Transfer brief
content.text: A teammate must be able to reproduce your conclusion without seeing the earlier answer choices. Name evidence and limits, not only the final verdict.

hints: Start the three-lookalike failure archive case note with the earliest observed event.
Use these three-lookalike failure archive technical anchors where they fit: URL, request, response, folder, relative path.
Close the three-lookalike failure archive note by naming one alternative and the evidence that rules it out.
